france.name: Atlantic influence
france.description: Atlantic weather moderates western France, bringing cooling influence, humidity, and vintage variation.

france.name: Mediterranean influence
france.description: The Mediterranean helps drive warmer, sunnier southern French growing conditions.

germany.name: Rhine corridor
germany.description: Major river corridors help reflect light and soften cool continental conditions in German vineyards.

austria.name: Danube River
austria.description: The Danube moderates temperature, reflects light, and shapes many of Austria's best vineyard sites.

greece.name: Aegean maritime influence
greece.description: The Aegean brings cooling winds and maritime moderation to many Greek vineyards.

italy.name: Alpine barrier
italy.description: The Alps provide shelter while altitude and valley exposures strongly shape northern Italian climates.

spain.name: Atlantic influence
spain.description: Atlantic conditions cool and moisten Spain's northwest and parts of the north.

spain.name: Mediterranean influence
spain.description: Mediterranean warmth raises temperatures and sunshine on Spain's eastern and southeastern side.

portugal.name: Atlantic influence
portugal.description: The Atlantic cools Portugal's western vineyards and increases humidity and rainfall near the coast.

usa.name: Pacific influence
usa.description: Cold Pacific influence, currents, and fog are central moderating factors for many West Coast vineyards.

chile.name: Humboldt Current
chile.description: The cold Humboldt Current cools Chile's coast, preserving freshness and slowing ripening in coastal zones.

chile.name: Andes influence
chile.description: The Andes create altitude, cool nights, and a rain-shadow effect that shape Chilean viticulture.

argentina.name: Andes influence
argentina.description: The Andes provide altitude, meltwater for irrigation, and strong day-night variation in Argentina's vineyards.

south-africa.name: Benguela Current
south-africa.description: The cold Benguela Current flows north up the west coast, cooling Cape vineyards and helping preserve acidity.

australia.name: Southern Ocean influence
australia.description: Southern Ocean air helps moderate many Australian regions, especially cooler coastal and island sites.

new-zealand.name: Tasman Sea influence
new-zealand.description: The Tasman Sea moderates western New Zealand and contributes to a cool maritime growing environment.

new-zealand.name: Pacific influence
new-zealand.description: The Pacific Ocean brings maritime moderation, wind, and freshness to many eastern New Zealand sites.

bordeaux.name: Atlantic influence
bordeaux.description: Atlantic weather cools Bordeaux, increases rainfall, and makes vintage variation important.

bordeaux.name: Gironde estuary
bordeaux.description: The Gironde, Garonne, and Dordogne moderate local temperatures and shape humidity patterns.

south-west-france.name: Atlantic transition
south-west-france.description: Atlantic influence is strongest in the west, while inland sectors become warmer and more varied.

burgundy.name: Slope exposure
burgundy.description: The best Burgundian vineyards rely heavily on hillside exposure and drainage rather than a broad maritime influence.

burgundy.name: River corridors
burgundy.description: The Yonne and Saone valleys help shape mesoclimate and site variation across Burgundy.

northern-rhone.name: Rhone River
northern-rhone.description: The Rhone corridor shapes vineyard exposure and local mesoclimate in the steep northern valley.

northern-rhone.name: Mistral
northern-rhone.description: The Mistral dries vineyards, reduces disease pressure, and can lower temperatures.

southern-rhone.name: Rhone River
southern-rhone.description: The Rhone valley forms a major corridor through the warm southern part of the region.

southern-rhone.name: Mistral
southern-rhone.description: The Mistral helps dry vineyards and can moderate the heat in Southern Rhone vineyards.

alsace.name: Vosges rain shadow
alsace.description: The Vosges reduce rainfall and help make Alsace notably drier and sunnier than many nearby regions.

champagne.name: Marne valley corridor
champagne.description: The Marne valley shapes exposure and contributes to local mesoclimate differences in Champagne.

provence.name: Mediterranean Sea
provence.description: Mediterranean influence brings warmth, sunlight, and sea moderation to Provence.

languedoc-roussillon.name: Mediterranean Sea
languedoc-roussillon.description: Mediterranean warmth is moderated in places by coastal exposure, wind, and altitude.

loire-valley.name: Loire River
loire-valley.description: The Loire and its tributaries moderate temperature and help create the valley’s key mesoclimates.

loire-valley.name: Atlantic influence
loire-valley.description: Atlantic influence is strongest in the west and gradually weakens as the valley runs inland.

mosel.name: Mosel River
mosel.description: The Mosel reflects light and moderates temperature in this very cool, steep river valley.

rheingau.name: Rhine River
rheingau.description: The Rhine reflects light and helps moderate temperatures for Rheingau vineyards.

nahe.name: Nahe River
nahe.description: River valleys and mixed exposures soften the climate across Nahe.

rheinhessen.name: Rhine influence
rheinhessen.description: The Rhine helps moderate temperature while sheltered sites add ripening potential.

pfalz.name: Haardt Mountains
pfalz.description: Mountain shelter reduces rainfall and helps make Pfalz warmer and drier than many German regions.

franken.name: Main River
franken.description: The Main river corridor and slope exposure help this inland region ripen fruit.

niederosterreich.name: Danube River
niederosterreich.description: The Danube moderates temperature and reflects light across Lower Austria.

burgenland.name: Lake Neusiedl
burgenland.description: Lake Neusiedl moderates temperature and promotes humidity and botrytis potential.

steiermark.name: Alpine cooling
steiermark.description: Altitude and Alpine influence help preserve freshness in Steiermark.

tokaj.name: Tisza and Bodrog rivers
tokaj.description: River mists encourage botrytis and are central to Tokaj's sweet wine production.

eger.name: Higher inland sites
eger.description: Altitude and inland continental conditions help keep Eger cooler than warmer southern Hungarian areas.

santorini.name: Aegean winds
santorini.description: Strong Aegean winds cool the island and explain the traditional basket-trained vines.

naoussa.name: Mountain influence
naoussa.description: Altitude and mountain influence help keep Naoussa cooler than many Mediterranean regions.

amyndeon.name: Altitude and lake influence
amyndeon.description: Altitude and nearby lakes help make Amyndeon one of Greece's cooler wine regions.

veneto.name: Lake Garda
veneto.description: Lake Garda helps moderate nearby vineyards and supports fresher styles in parts of Veneto.

veneto.name: Alpine protection
veneto.description: The Alps shelter parts of Veneto and create useful site variation.

tuscany.name: Tyrrhenian influence
tuscany.description: Sea influence and inland hills help moderate temperature across Tuscany.

marche.name: Adriatic influence
marche.description: The Adriatic helps moderate Marche, especially for white-wine areas near the coast.

umbria.name: Apennine shelter
umbria.description: Inland hills and Apennine influence help Umbria keep cooler nights and preserve freshness.

lazio.name: Tyrrhenian influence
lazio.description: Coastal influence and elevated volcanic sites help moderate Lazio.

abruzzo.name: Adriatic influence
abruzzo.description: The Adriatic and hillside elevation shifts help moderate Abruzzo and preserve freshness.

campania.name: Tyrrhenian influence
campania.description: Sea influence and altitude are important moderating factors in Campania.

puglia.name: Adriatic influence
puglia.description: Sea influence helps moderate heat in parts of Puglia despite the region's warm, sunny climate.

basilicata.name: Volcanic elevation
basilicata.description: Elevation around Mount Vulture is the key moderating factor in Basilicata, helping Aglianico retain acidity.

sicily.name: Sea breezes
sicily.description: Sea breezes help moderate Sicily, while altitude is critical around Etna.

piemonte.name: Alpine shelter
piemonte.description: Alpine shelter, hills, and aspect are important ripening factors in Piemonte.

trentino-alto-adige.name: Alpine influence
trentino-alto-adige.description: Altitude, Alpine shelter, and valley exposures strongly shape this cool northern area.

friuli-venezia-giulia.name: Alpine shelter
friuli-venezia-giulia.description: Alpine shelter and elevation help shape the region’s cool nights and fresh white-wine styles.

friuli-venezia-giulia.name: Adriatic influence
friuli-venezia-giulia.description: The nearby Adriatic helps moderate temperatures and preserve freshness in Friuli’s vineyards.

rias-baixas.name: Atlantic influence
rias-baixas.description: Strong Atlantic influence keeps Rias Baixas cool, wet, and well ventilated.

rioja.name: Ebro corridor
rioja.description: The Ebro corridor and altitude differences help create Rioja's subregional variation.

rioja.name: Mountain shelter
rioja.description: Mountain shelter helps protect Rioja from harsher weather while Atlantic influence remains important in cooler zones.

ribera-del-duero.name: Duero River
ribera-del-duero.description: The Duero corridor and high elevation produce a marked diurnal range in Ribera del Duero.

rueda.name: High plateau altitude
rueda.description: Elevation on the Castilian plateau helps Rueda retain freshness despite warm sunny days.

priorat.name: Elevated slate slopes
priorat.description: Altitude and steep poor slate slopes help moderate Priorat's warmth and concentrate the wines.

jerez.name: Atlantic influence
jerez.description: Atlantic breezes moderate Jerez and are important for flor and Sherry ageing conditions.

penedes.name: Mediterranean influence
penedes.description: Mediterranean warmth is moderated by altitude in higher Penedes sites.

navarra.name: North-south transition
navarra.description: Navarra bridges cooler Atlantic influence in the north and warmer Mediterranean conditions in the south.

catalunya.name: Mediterranean influence
catalunya.description: Mediterranean conditions dominate Catalunya, though inland altitude helps diversify styles.

la-mancha.name: High Meseta altitude
la-mancha.description: La Mancha's elevated inland plateau helps preserve some freshness in this otherwise hot dry region.

jumilla.name: Altitude moderation
jumilla.description: Altitude helps moderate this otherwise hot, dry inland region.

valencia.name: Mediterranean influence
valencia.description: Coastal influence moderates parts of Valencia, while inland areas are warmer.

vinho-verde.name: Atlantic influence
vinho-verde.description: Atlantic influence keeps Vinho Verde cool, wet, and relatively low in alcohol.

douro.name: Douro River
douro.description: The Douro river corridor and terraced slopes create major mesoclimate differences across the valley.

dao.name: Mountain protection
dao.description: Surrounding mountains shelter Dao and help shape a more moderate inland climate.

california.name: Pacific cooling
california.description: Cold Pacific influence and fog are core moderating factors in California viticulture.

oregon.name: Pacific influence
oregon.description: Pacific influence helps keep Oregon relatively cool with a long growing season.

washington-state.name: Cascade rain shadow
washington-state.description: The Cascades create a rain shadow, giving eastern Washington warm, dry conditions with cool nights.

aconcagua.name: Pacific cooling
aconcagua.description: Pacific breezes and the cold Humboldt Current moderate the western side of Aconcagua.

central-valley.name: Andes cooling
central-valley.description: Andes influence helps cool nights and maintain freshness in inland Central Valley vineyards.

central-valley.name: Pacific influence
central-valley.description: Pacific air tempers Chile's warm inland valleys, especially in more western zones.

mendoza.name: Andes altitude
mendoza.description: Altitude and meltwater from the Andes are crucial to Mendoza's viticulture and day-night temperature range.

salta.name: Extreme altitude
salta.description: Extreme altitude cools nights and slows ripening in one of the world's highest vineyard areas.

patagonia.name: Cool southern winds
patagonia.description: Southern latitude and persistent winds help keep Patagonia markedly cooler.

stellenbosch.name: Cape sea breezes
stellenbosch.description: Ocean breezes from False Bay and nearby cold-water influence help moderate Stellenbosch.

paarl.name: Cape cooling influence
paarl.description: Paarl is warmer inland, but still receives some moderating ocean influence from the Cape.

walker-bay.name: Cold ocean influence
walker-bay.description: Walker Bay is strongly cooled by nearby ocean influence, helping Pinot Noir and Chardonnay ripen slowly.

swartland.name: Benguela cooling
swartland.description: The Benguela current helps moderate heat in parts of Swartland, though the region remains dry and warm.

barossa.name: Elevated Eden influence
barossa.description: Higher, cooler nearby sites and local elevation differences help balance warmer Barossa conditions.

mclaren-vale.name: Gulf sea breezes
mclaren-vale.description: Sea breezes from Gulf St Vincent help moderate McLaren Vale's warm climate.

adelaide-hills.name: Altitude
adelaide-hills.description: Altitude is the key moderating factor that keeps Adelaide Hills cooler and fresher.

yarra-valley.name: Ocean influence
yarra-valley.description: Ocean influence helps keep Yarra Valley cooler than many inland Australian regions.

hunter-valley.name: Pacific influence
hunter-valley.description: Pacific influence and cloud cover help temper the Hunter Valley's warm, humid climate.

margaret-river.name: Ocean influence
margaret-river.description: Margaret River is strongly moderated by surrounding ocean influence, helping a long even ripening season.

tasmania.name: Bass Strait and Southern Ocean
tasmania.description: Cool maritime influence from Bass Strait and the Southern Ocean keeps Tasmania one of Australia's coolest regions.

rutherglen.name: Long warm ripening season
rutherglen.description: Rutherglen stays warm and sunny, allowing very ripe fruit for fortified Muscat production.

marlborough.name: Cook Strait airflow
marlborough.description: Cool maritime airflow and a large diurnal range help drive Marlborough's pungent aromatic style.

hawkes-bay.name: Pacific influence
hawkes-bay.description: Pacific influence moderates Hawke's Bay, though it remains one of New Zealand's warmer regions.

central-otago.name: Mountain shelter
central-otago.description: Mountain shelter makes Central Otago drier and more continental than most of New Zealand.

wairarapa.name: Cook Strait winds
wairarapa.description: Wind and maritime influence help moderate Wairarapa and Martinborough.

medoc.name: Gironde estuary
medoc.description: The Gironde estuary moderates Medoc and helps support Cabernet Sauvignon ripening in this maritime zone.

haut-medoc.name: Gironde estuary
haut-medoc.description: The Gironde and nearby waterways help moderate Haut-Medoc and reduce temperature extremes.

margaux.name: Estuary moderation
margaux.description: Margaux benefits from estuary influence that helps preserve balance in this maritime appellation.

pauillac.name: Estuary moderation
pauillac.description: The nearby Gironde helps Pauillac ripen Cabernet Sauvignon while keeping a measured maritime balance.

saint-julien.name: Estuary moderation
saint-julien.description: Saint-Julien's proximity to the Gironde helps moderate temperatures and support even ripening.

saint-estephe.name: Estuary moderation
saint-estephe.description: The Gironde helps soften Saint-Estephe's cool maritime conditions, though the commune remains one of the firmer Medoc styles.

graves.name: Garonne corridor
graves.description: The Garonne and nearby river corridor help moderate Graves and shape the local mesoclimate.

pessac-leognan.name: Garonne corridor
pessac-leognan.description: The Garonne corridor helps moderate Pessac-Leognan while supporting both red and white production.

pomerol.name: Right Bank river influence
pomerol.description: Nearby Dordogne-side moderation helps soften temperature extremes in Pomerol's inland Right Bank setting.

saint-emilion.name: Right Bank river influence
saint-emilion.description: The Dordogne corridor contributes some moderation around Saint-Emilion despite its more inland setting.

sauternes.name: Ciron and Garonne
sauternes.description: The meeting of the Ciron and Garonne creates the humid autumn conditions that favor botrytis in Sauternes.

barsac.name: Ciron and Garonne
barsac.description: Mist from the Ciron and Garonne helps Barsac develop the botrytis conditions central to its sweet wines.

chablis.name: Serein valley
chablis.description: The Serein valley gives some local moderation to Chablis, but the area remains distinctly cool and marginal.

cote-dor.name: East-facing escarpment
cote-dor.description: The east-facing limestone escarpment is the crucial moderating and quality factor in the Cote d'Or.

cote-chalonnaise.name: Limestone hills
cote-chalonnaise.description: Hillside exposures and limestone slopes help the Cote Chalonnaise ripen fruit in a cool continental setting.

maconnais.name: Southern hillside warmth
maconnais.description: The Maconnais benefits from warmer southern conditions while hillside sites still help preserve balance.

beaujolais.name: Granite hills
beaujolais.description: The rolling granitic hills are a key moderating and quality factor in Beaujolais.

muscadet.name: Atlantic influence
muscadet.description: Strong Atlantic influence keeps Muscadet cool and supports high-acid, light-bodied wines.

anjou-saumur.name: Loire River
anjou-saumur.description: The Loire helps moderate temperature and supports the region's varied sparkling, white, and rosé styles.

touraine.name: Loire River
touraine.description: The Loire corridor adds moderation while inland influence increases toward the east.

touraine/vouvray.name: Loire River
touraine/vouvray.description: The Loire helps moderate Vouvray’s cool Chenin Blanc vineyards and supports balanced ripening.

touraine/chinon.name: Loire River
touraine/chinon.description: The Loire corridor and sheltered valley sites help Cabernet Franc ripen while preserving freshness.

touraine/bourgueil.name: Loire River
touraine/bourgueil.description: The Loire corridor moderates Bourgueil and helps retain freshness in Cabernet Franc.

central-vineyards.name: Loire River
central-vineyards.description: The Loire and its tributaries help moderate this otherwise cool inland part of the valley.

central-vineyards/sancerre.name: Loire River
central-vineyards/sancerre.description: The Loire corridor gives some moderation to this cool inland Sauvignon Blanc zone.

central-vineyards/pouilly-fume.name: Loire River
central-vineyards/pouilly-fume.description: The Loire and local mesoclimates help shape ripe yet fresh Sauvignon Blanc in Pouilly-Fume.

cote-rotie.name: Steep Rhone slopes
cote-rotie.description: The steep Rhone-side slopes are essential for ripening Syrah in Cote-Rotie.

condrieu.name: Steep river slopes
condrieu.description: Condrieu's steep slopes above the Rhone help warm and ripen Viognier while retaining some freshness.

saint-joseph.name: Rhone corridor
saint-joseph.description: The Rhone corridor moderates Saint-Joseph and shapes its long narrow north-south vineyard strip.

crozes-hermitage.name: Rhone corridor
crozes-hermitage.description: The Rhone corridor helps moderate Crozes-Hermitage, though the flatter terrain gives a broader range of exposures.

hermitage.name: South-facing hill
hermitage.description: Hermitage's famous south-facing hill above the Rhone is the key moderating and quality factor.

cornas.name: Sheltered granite slopes
cornas.description: Cornas is protected by its steep sheltered slopes, making it one of the warmer Northern Rhone Syrah sites.

chateauneuf-du-pape.name: Mistral
chateauneuf-du-pape.description: The Mistral helps dry Chateauneuf-du-Pape and slightly tempers the heat in this very warm appellation.

gigondas.name: Dentelles foothills
gigondas.description: Foothill elevation below the Dentelles helps Gigondas retain more freshness than the warm plain.

vacqueyras.name: Dentelles foothills
vacqueyras.description: Slightly elevated foothill sites help Vacqueyras balance Southern Rhone ripeness with freshness.

tavel.name: Mistral
tavel.description: The Mistral is an important drying and moderating factor in warm Tavel.

beaumes-de-venise.name: Dentelles foothills
beaumes-de-venise.description: Foothill sites near the Dentelles help Beaumes-de-Venise retain some freshness in a warm Mediterranean setting.

montagne-de-reims.name: Slope exposure
montagne-de-reims.description: Hillside exposure is central in the Montagne de Reims, helping grapes ripen in this cool region.

vallee-de-la-marne.name: Marne River
vallee-de-la-marne.description: The Marne valley provides local moderation and shapes the mesoclimate of this important Champagne sub-region.

cote-des-blancs.name: Chalky slope exposure
cote-des-blancs.description: The best south- and southeast-facing chalk slopes help the Cote des Blancs ripen Chardonnay while retaining high acidity.

cote-des-bar.name: Southern slope influence
cote-des-bar.description: The warmer more southerly position and hillside exposures help make the Cote des Bar riper than northern Champagne sectors.

alto-adige.name: Alpine altitude
alto-adige.description: Altitude and Alpine valley exposures keep Alto Adige cool and help preserve aromatic purity.

trentino.name: Alpine valley influence
trentino.description: Mountain valleys and elevation help Trentino retain freshness despite strong sunshine.

friuli-grave.name: Adriatic influence
friuli-grave.description: Adriatic breezes and the broad gravel plain help Friuli Grave produce fresh, clean white wines.

collio.name: Alpine-Adriatic balance
collio.description: Sheltered hills between the Alps and Adriatic help Collio ripen grapes fully while retaining freshness.

colli-orientali-del-friuli.name: Sheltered eastern hills
colli-orientali-del-friuli.description: Hillside sites and Alpine shelter help Colli Orientali del Friuli give concentrated yet fresh wines.

valpolicella.name: Lake Garda influence
valpolicella.description: Lake Garda and nearby foothills help moderate Valpolicella and preserve freshness in Corvina.

soave.name: Eastern hillside sites
soave.description: Hillside vineyards and cooler exposures help Soave retain acidity and definition.

conegliano-valdobbiadene.name: Cool hillside exposures
conegliano-valdobbiadene.description: Steeper cooler hillside sites help Conegliano-Valdobbiadene keep freshness and finesse.

chianti-classico.name: Inland hill altitude
chianti-classico.description: Elevated inland hills help Chianti Classico retain acidity and freshness in Sangiovese.

montalcino.name: Hill elevation
montalcino.description: Elevated slopes and day-night shifts help Montalcino balance ripe fruit with structure.

montepulciano.name: Hill altitude
montepulciano.description: Elevated Tuscan hill sites help Montepulciano preserve freshness and structure.

bolgheri.name: Tyrrhenian influence
bolgheri.description: Sea influence helps moderate warm coastal Bolgheri and supports more balanced ripening.

verdicchio-dei-castelli-di-jesi.name: Adriatic influence
verdicchio-dei-castelli-di-jesi.description: The Adriatic helps keep Castelli di Jesi fresher and well suited to vibrant Verdicchio.

verdicchio-di-matelica.name: Inland valley diurnal range
verdicchio-di-matelica.description: Matelica's inland valley setting brings cooler nights and firmer acidity than many coastal Marche sites.

orvieto.name: Inland hill influence
orvieto.description: Elevated inland sites help Orvieto retain more freshness than the warm central Italian location might suggest.

montefalco.name: Hill elevation
montefalco.description: Warm hill sites and day-night variation help Montefalco ripen structured reds without losing all freshness.

frascati.name: Volcanic hill sites
frascati.description: Elevated volcanic hills and nearby Tyrrhenian influence help Frascati stay fresher than the surrounding lowlands.

montepulciano-dabruzzo.name: Adriatic influence
montepulciano-dabruzzo.description: Adriatic moderation and hillside sites help Montepulciano d'Abruzzo keep balance in a warm region.

trebbiano-dabruzzo.name: Adriatic cooling
trebbiano-dabruzzo.description: Sea influence and altitude variation help Trebbiano d'Abruzzo preserve freshness and acidity.

taurasi.name: Inland altitude
taurasi.description: Elevated inland sites are crucial in Taurasi, helping Aglianico retain acidity and structure.

fiano-di-avellino.name: Hill elevation
fiano-di-avellino.description: Altitude is a key moderating factor in Fiano di Avellino, preserving freshness in this southern white appellation.

greco-di-tufo.name: Elevated inland sites
greco-di-tufo.description: Higher inland vineyards help Greco di Tufo keep its notable acidity and mineral precision.

primitivo-di-manduria.name: Salento sea breezes
primitivo-di-manduria.description: Sea breezes in the Salento help temper the heat around Manduria, though styles remain ripe and powerful.

salice-salentino.name: Peninsular sea influence
salice-salentino.description: Sea influence helps Salice Salentino avoid even greater heat in this warm southern peninsula.

aglianico-del-vulture.name: Volcanic altitude
aglianico-del-vulture.description: Mount Vulture's elevation is the defining moderating factor for Aglianico del Vulture.

etna.name: Etna altitude
etna.description: High elevations on Etna are the key moderating factor behind Sicily's fresher, more elegant volcanic styles.

marsala.name: Coastal breezes
marsala.description: Western coastal breezes help moderate Marsala's hot conditions and shape its historic fortified-wine zone.

barolo.name: Langhe hill sites
barolo.description: Elevated Langhe slopes and Alpine shelter are essential for Nebbiolo ripening in Barolo.

barbaresco.name: Langhe hill sites
barbaresco.description: Sheltered hills and continental moderation help Barbaresco ripen Nebbiolo while keeping freshness.

asti.name: Hillside exposure
asti.description: Hillside vineyards help Asti retain freshness and aromatic precision for Moscato-based styles.

gavi.name: Sheltered southeastern hills
gavi.description: Carefully sited hillside vineyards help Gavi preserve Cortese's freshness and delicacy.

val-do-salnes.name: Atlantic exposure
val-do-salnes.description: Strong Atlantic exposure helps Val do Salnes produce especially fresh saline Albarino.

o-rosal.name: Atlantic breezes
o-rosal.description: Atlantic breezes keep O Rosal fresh and well ventilated despite humid conditions.

rioja-alta.name: Atlantic-influenced western sites
rioja-alta.description: Cooler western exposures and the Ebro corridor help Rioja Alta keep elegance and freshness.

rioja-alavesa.name: Sierra Cantabria shelter
rioja-alavesa.description: Shelter from the Sierra Cantabria helps Rioja Alavesa retain freshness while protecting vineyards from harsher weather.

rioja-oriental.name: Warmer eastern Ebro sites
rioja-oriental.description: The lower warmer eastern stretch of Rioja gives Rioja Oriental a riper style despite some river moderation.

alt-penedes.name: Higher-altitude inland sites
alt-penedes.description: Higher inland vineyards are crucial for fresher fruit and sparkling base wines in Alt Penedes.

sant-sadurni-danoia.name: Elevated inland setting
sant-sadurni-danoia.description: Slightly higher inland conditions around Sant Sadurni d'Anoia help preserve the freshness needed for Cava.

baix-penedes.name: Mediterranean coastal influence
baix-penedes.description: Baix Penedes is warmer and more maritime, with stronger Mediterranean influence than the higher inland sectors.

jerez-de-la-frontera.name: Atlantic breezes
jerez-de-la-frontera.description: Atlantic influence still reaches inland Jerez de la Frontera and helps shape ageing conditions for Sherry.

sanlucar-de-barrameda.name: Strong Atlantic humidity
sanlucar-de-barrameda.description: Direct Atlantic humidity makes Sanlucar especially favorable for persistent flor and Manzanilla style.

el-puerto-de-santa-maria.name: Maritime Atlantic influence
el-puerto-de-santa-maria.description: Strong maritime influence helps El Puerto de Santa Maria maintain flor-friendly ageing conditions.

wachau.name: Danube River
wachau.description: The Danube is a central moderating factor in the Wachau, alongside stone terraces and diurnal range.

lujan-de-cuyo.name: Andes altitude
lujan-de-cuyo.description: Moderate elevation near the Andes helps balance warm sunny days with cool nights.

uco-valley.name: High-altitude Andes sites
uco-valley.description: Very high altitude is a defining moderating factor in the Uco Valley.

casablanca.name: Pacific fog
casablanca.description: Cold Pacific fog and breezes make Casablanca a distinctly cooler coastal region.

rapel-valley/colchagua.name: Andes cooling
rapel-valley/colchagua.description: Andean air and altitude influence help preserve balance in warmer parts of Colchagua.

napa-valley.name: San Pablo Bay
napa-valley.description: Cool air and fog from San Pablo Bay moderate the south of Napa and influence valley mesoclimates.

sonoma.name: Pacific fog
sonoma.description: Pacific fog and cool-air corridors are key moderating factors across Sonoma.

central-coast.name: Pacific influence
central-coast.description: Ocean air, fog, and gaps in the coastal ranges strongly moderate the Central Coast.
